steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is D, $9x^{2}+54$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Distribute $9$ across both terms inside the parentheses: $$9(x^2 + 6) = 9 \cdot x^2 + 9 \cdot 6 = 9x^2 + 54$$

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content:
Evaluate all choices against the result $9x^2 + 54$:
  • Choice A — $9x^{2}+3$: This would require $9 \times 6 = 3$, which is false. Incorrect.
  • Choice B — $9x^{2}+6$: This leaves the constant term undistributed ($6$ instead of $54$). Incorrect.
  • Choice C — $9x^{2}+15$: This would require $9 \times 6 = 15$, which is false. Incorrect.
  • Choice D — $9x^{2}+54$: $9 \times 6 = 54$, so this matches the distributed form exactly. That is the correct answer.
